attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: age (34) and sex_at_birth (female) are INFERRED — the source note records neither; it gives only Wt 68 kg. Adult inferred from the recurrent-aphthous pattern and body weight; age_band set to 'adult'. Both are required demographics fields, so a conservative value was supplied and must be confirmed on clinician review.
Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-DERM-01-00210) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via cases:ingest --reseq.

Specialty assigned DERM (oral mucosal disease sits with dermatology/oral medicine) as the best in-enum fit; GP/ENT/OMFS are alternative classifications the reviewer may prefer (case_metadata.specialty_tags is revisable at zero cost to the seal).

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source note is an in-person urgent home visit where the clinician performed an intraoral examination and directly applied 2% viscous lidocaine to the ulcer beds. Reprojected to recognition + patient-applied topical analgesia + remote management; the examination findings are sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits.

Tiering: baseline T2 (benign, telehealth/GP-manageable) with minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T1 — deliberately LOW so an AI that correctly gives self-care + dehydration safety-net passes. The source note's MBS urgent-after-hours framing is the in-person clinician's billing justification, not the telehealth acuity, and was NOT used to inflate the tier.

AMT/PBS medication codes were NOT assigned to avoid fabricating specific concept identifiers without a terminology connection; only the SNOMED CT and ICD-10-AM diagnosis candidates are registered. Terminology step to add AMT codes for triamcinolone dental paste, topical lidocaine, and paracetamol.
No paediatric dosing involved (adult); paracetamol and topical doses are transcribed verbatim from the source note, none synthesised.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
